name: Web Intl Conformance Demo
summary: The runnable proof that the CustomIntlProvider contract (#1054) is conformant. The runtime (the native-first Intl.* provider + customIntl swap registry) is relocated to Frontier UI (#1914, WE holds zero executable); its conformance runs through the plateau-hosted conformance runner (#1788/#1790) driving the WE-owned intl vector suite (#1917: formatToParts part-type sequence + Collator.compare sign, #1816) against the FUI binding. The pass/fail panel is rendered at plateau-origin inside a cross-origin iframe.
description:
Concept

The runnable proof that the CustomIntlProvider contract (#1054) is conformant. The runtime — the native-first Intl.* provider and the customIntl swap registry — is relocated to Frontier UI (#1914; WE holds zero executable, #1282). Its conformance runs through the plateau-hosted conformance runner (#1788 / #1790), which drives the WE intl vector suite (#1917: formatToParts part-type sequence + Collator.compare sign) against the FUI binding.

Verified contract

WE owns only the build-agnostic vectors and the type-only CustomIntlProvider contract; the provider impl is FUI's, the runner is plateau's. The pass/fail panel is rendered at plateau-origin inside an iframe — the cross-origin boundary (#1788 ratified (b)) that reaches the shared, neutral runner with no implementer→runner edge. A green result proves conformance independent of how the runtime is built (#899).
